title: AI Strategy: 4 Ways ROI & Your First Project
plainEnglishSummary: Where AI pays off for busy owners — and how to pick one first project that actually sticks.

reading.script:
Let's talk strategy — where AI actually pays off for a busy owner, and how to pick a first project that sticks instead of becoming shelfware.

There are four ways AI creates ROI. One — saves time: drafts, summaries, first passes on email, content, notes. Two — saves money: less contractor time on repetitive copy, research, or admin prep. Three — makes money: faster follow-up, more content, better lead response through speed and volume. Four — better decisions: clearer options, pros and cons, scenario planning from your data. You still decide — AI just helps you think faster.

How do you choose your first project? Pick something you do weekly, that takes thirty-plus minutes, and that doesn't require perfect accuracy on day one. Avoid starting with "AI everything" or a customer-facing bot with no guardrails. Win once, document it, then expand.

That's the whole game at this stage. One win beats ten experiments. Alright — activity time.

sections.heading: 4 ways AI creates ROI
paragraphs: Saves time — drafts, summaries, first passes on work you'd do manually: email, content, notes.
Saves money — less contractor time on repetitive copy, research, or admin prep.
Makes money — faster follow-up, more content, better lead response through speed and volume.
Better decisions — clearer options, pros and cons, scenario planning from your data. You still decide.

sections.heading: How to choose your first AI project
paragraphs: Pick something you do weekly, that takes thirty-plus minutes, and that doesn't require perfect accuracy on day one.
Avoid starting with "AI everything" or a customer-facing bot with no guardrails.
Win once, document it, then expand.

discussPrompts: Which of the 4 ROI types matters most to you right now?
What's your first AI project for the next 30 days?

steps.situation: You spend 45 min every Monday turning messy notes into a team update email.
steps.question: Primary ROI type?
steps.feedback: Weekly time sink → classic time-save project. AI drafts, you edit 5 min.

steps.situation: You want AI to approve refunds over $500 automatically.
steps.question: Good first project?
options: No — high stakes, needs human judgment first
Yes — if you use the most expensive model
steps.feedback: Money + customer trust = not your first pilot.

steps.situation: Cold leads sit 3 days before anyone follows up. You want faster personalized outreach.
steps.question: Primary ROI type?
steps.feedback: Speed to lead = revenue. AI drafts; human sends.

steps.situation: You're choosing your FIRST AI project as a busy owner.
steps.question: Best pick?
options: Weekly task, 30+ min, low accuracy risk, you can review output
Full autonomous customer service with no review
Replace your CRM in one weekend
steps.feedback: Small weekly win beats big risky automation.

steps.situation: You pasted last quarter's numbers and want AI to suggest 3 strategic options with tradeoffs.
steps.question: Primary ROI type?
steps.feedback: Scenario framing from your data — you decide. AI assists thinking.

steps.situation: You hired a copywriter for $2k/mo mostly for social post first drafts.
steps.question: AI might…
options: Save Money on first drafts — keep human for brand polish
Replace all marketing forever with no review
steps.feedback: AI drafts → less contractor hours on repetitive work.

takeaway: ROI = time, money, revenue, or decisions. First project = weekly, reviewable, low risk.
